content: Hey, I'm feeling really stuck in my career. I've been working as a Fashion Buyer for a luxury department store in Pittsburgh for about 3 years now.
I'm sorry to hear that you're feeling stuck. Can you tell me a bit more about what's not fulfilling about your current role?

Well, I used to love my job when I was working in the Bay Area, but since I moved to Pittsburgh, I've been feeling really disconnected from the fashion scene. I miss the energy of the city and the opportunity to attend fashion events.

That makes sense. It can be tough to adjust to a new city, especially when it's a big change from what you're used to. Have you considered looking for a new job in Pittsburgh that might be a better fit for you?

Yeah, I've been thinking about it. I've also been considering starting my own business, maybe something related to sustainable fashion. I've been really passionate about that lately and I think it could be a great opportunity.
That sounds like an exciting idea! Starting your own business can be a lot of work, but it can also be very rewarding. What kind of experience do you have with sustainable fashion?

I've been learning about it in my free time and I've even started a small project on the side, creating my own sustainable fashion line. It's been a lot of fun and I've learned so much. My friend, Rachel, has been a big help, she's a designer and has given me some great advice.
That's great to hear! It's always helpful to have a support system when starting a new venture. What kind of goals do you have for your sustainable fashion line?

I'd love to have it up and running by the end of the year, maybe even have a small pop-up shop in Pittsburgh. I've been looking at a few different locations, including the Strip District and Shadyside.

Those are both great locations! The Strip District is always bustling with activity and Shadyside has a great mix of shops and restaurants. What kind of products are you planning to sell in your pop-up shop?

I'm planning to sell a mix of clothing and accessories, all made from sustainable materials. I've been experimenting with different designs and I'm really excited about how they're turning out. I've even been able to incorporate some of my other hobbies, like origami, into the designs.
That sounds like a great way to combine your passions. Origami is a unique skill, how did you get into it?

I've just always been fascinated by it, I love the idea of creating something beautiful out of nothing. I've been practicing for years and I've even taught a few classes on the side. It's been a great way to relax and express my creativity.
That's wonderful. It's great that you've found a way to combine your passions and hobbies into your career. I think you have a lot of potential for success with your sustainable fashion line.
